why_better_than_alternatives: The completed Υ-C band removed the specific control-plane ambiguity that previously kept Φ-B4 dangerous.
Moving to Φ-B4 now resolves the next real missing foundation instead of continuing to layer release doctrine ahead of runtime boundary law.
Jumping directly to Φ-B5 would still outrun distributed trust and authority maturity.

blocked_or_dangerous_items.summary: Distributed authority remains blocked by missing authority handoff, replication proof, and trust convergence maturity.
Live trust rotation and revocation propagation are not the selected next block and remain under stronger gates.
Live-cutover receipt and runtime continuity generalization still depends on the output of Φ-B4.
Publication and trust operationalization remain too immature to schedule ahead of Φ-B4.
